title: Augmented Human Development Index
subtitle: The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.
caption:
Augmented Human Development Index

The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.

This choropleth map displays the Augmented Human Development Index for the year 2020, using a color scale ranging from 0 to 0.8, where darker shades of blue represent higher index values. The highest values, falling within the 0.7 to 0.8 band, are visible in Australia, New Zealand, Canada, the United States, Japan, South Korea, and a cluster of nations in Western and Northern Europe including Norway, Sweden, and Germany. Moving to the next tier, countries in the 0.6 to 0.7 range include much of Eastern Europe, Russia, China, and several nations in South America such as Argentina, Chile, and Uruguay. A broad middle band of 0.4 to 0.6 covers Mexico, Brazil, Turkey, and parts of Southeast Asia like Thailand and Malaysia. Lower values, situated between 0.2 and 0.4, are prevalent across much of South Asia, including India and Pakistan, as well as large portions of Sub-Saharan Africa and North Africa. The lowest recorded bands, between 0 and 0.2, appear in specific countries within Central Africa, such as the Central African Republic and Chad, alongside Yemen and Afghanistan. Greenland is marked with a hatched pattern, indicating that no data is available for this region. The visual distribution shows a concentration of higher index scores in the Americas, Europe, and Oceania, while lower scores are clustered across Africa and parts of Asia.
